cards.front: What is the primary purpose of hyperparameter tuning?
cards.back: To optimize model performance by adjusting the parameters of a machine learning algorithm

cards.front: What is the difference between model training and model validation?
cards.back: Model training involves training a model on a large dataset to learn the relationships between the inputs and outputs, while model validation involves evaluating the model on a separate dataset to estimate its generalization performance
A technique used to evaluate the performance of a model by splitting the data into multiple subsets and evaluating the model on each subset

cards.front: What is the purpose of a validation set in machine learning?
cards.back: To evaluate the model's performance on unseen data and estimate its generalization ability

cards.front: Why is it important to use a stratified split when splitting data for hyperparameter tuning?
cards.back: To ensure that the training and validation sets have the same class distribution as the original data, which is important for evaluating model performance on unseen data
